member.name: Sally Jameson
member.synopsis: Sally Jameson is the Labour (Co-op) MP for Doncaster Central , and has been an MP continually since 4 July 2024.

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: Energy: Prices
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero, what assessment he has made of the potential impact of the conflict in the Middle East on domestic energy bills.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The conflict in the Middle East demonstrates once again the need to get off the fossil fuel rollercoaster and drive for homegrown clean power that we control. As we drive forward with this agenda, we have also taken immediate action, including by removing...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero

writtenQuestions.heading: New Businesses
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Business and Trade, what steps his Department is taking to support entrepreneurs.
writtenQuestions.answerText: We are committed to delivering the measures outlined in the Small Business Plan including removing barriers to entrepreneurs, through the most significant package of legislative reforms in 25 years to tackle late payments.My department also supports entre...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Business and Trade

writtenQuestions.heading: Coalfields Regeneration Trust: Finance

writtenQuestions.answerText: I refer the hon. Member to the answer given to Question UIN 117179 on 10 March 2026.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government

writtenQuestions.heading: Coal: Regeneration
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Housing, Communities and Local Government, what assessment his Department has made of the potential growth benefits of capital investment in SME industrial units in former coalfield communities, including proposals put fo
writtenQuestions.answerText: This government is committed to ensuring that coalfield communities across Britain build stronger, brighter futures. Our Pride in Place strategy sets out how we will deliver up to £5.8 billion over ten years to 284 neighbourhoods experiencing the highest ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government

writtenQuestions.heading: Council Tax
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Housing, Communities and Local Government, if he will take steps to make levels of council tax equal in all local authority areas; and if he will make an assessment of the potential impact of doing so on councils with les
writtenQuestions.answerText: Council tax is managed by local authorities, who decide what level of council tax they wish to set. This will reflect the service needs of each area having taken account of their other sources of income. It will also reflect historic council tax decisions...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government

writtenQuestions.heading: Local Government Finance
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Housing, Communities and Local Government, if he will make it his policy to have a three-year transition period for the implementation of the fair funding settlement.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The government is carefully considering views expressed by the public, local authorities and other bodies on the proposals set out in the Fair Funding Review 2.0 consultation, including on the application of a remoteness adjustment, the distribution of gr...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government

writtenQuestions.answerText: The government is carefully considering views expressed by the public, local authorities and other bodies on the proposals set out in the Fair Funding Review 2.0 consultation, including on the application of a remoteness adjustment, the distribution of gr...

writtenQuestions.answerText: The government is carefully considering views expressed by the public, local authorities and other bodies on the proposals set out in the Fair Funding Review 2.0 consultation, including on the application of a remoteness adjustment, the distribution of gr...

writtenQuestions.answerText: The government is carefully considering views expressed by the public, local authorities and other bodies on the proposals set out in the Fair Funding Review 2.0 consultation, including on the application of a remoteness adjustment, the distribution of gr...

writtenQuestions.heading: Local Government Finance

writtenQuestions.heading: Hydrogen: Manufacturing Industries
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero, what assessment his Department has made of the potential impact of the hydrogen technology manufacturing sector on (a) investment to the UK and (b) the growth in good jobs.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The hydrogen sector is nascent but is expected to grow significantly; the global hydrogen market could exceed $1 trillion by 2050, with the UK well positioned to capture a substantial share. We intend to publish a revised Hydrogen Strategy which will high...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero

writtenQuestions.heading: Hydrogen: Exports
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Business and Trade, what steps he is taking to support the hydrogen technology manufacturing sector to export (a) goods and (b) services.
writtenQuestions.answerText: As a frontier Industrial Strategy sector, the government has committed targeted support to boost exports of hydrogen sector goods and services by showcasing UK capabilities through our global network, providing greater access to international supply chain...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Business and Trade

writtenQuestions.heading: Hydrogen: Manufacturing Industries
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Business and Trade, what steps he is taking to encourage investment into the hydrogen technology manufacturing sector.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Under the Industrial Strategy's Clean Energy Industries Sector Plan the Government announced a public finance offer to crowd private investment into clean energy industries, including the UK's hydrogen economy. This includes: a £1bn Clean Energy supply ch...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Business and Trade

writtenQuestions.heading: Hydrogen
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero, when he plans to publish the consultation on the expansion of the Clean Industry Bonus to the hydrogen sector.
writtenQuestions.answerText: As set out in the Clean Energy Industries Sector Plan, we are considering expanding the Clean Industry Bonus to hydrogen and will consult on any proposals. We are currently developing a new Hydrogen Strategy and will provide an update on this in due cours...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero

writtenQuestions.heading: Hydrogen: Innovation and Research
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero, what steps he is taking to encourage (a) innovation and (b) research and development in the hydrogen technology manufacturing sector.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department has allocated around £170 million from the £1bn Net Zero Innovation Portfolio to encourage hydrogen R&D, including £60m for Low Carbon Hydrogen Supply 2 and £31m for H2BECCS, to support production, storage, transport, and negative-emiss...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero

writtenQuestions.heading: Hydrogen: Regulation
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero, what steps he is taking through (a) regulatory reform and (b) market incentives to support the growth of the hydrogen technology manufacturing sector.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The UK is optimally positioned to develop a thriving hydrogen economy, with many UK companies at the forefront of hydrogen technology advancement. The Government has announced a public finance offer for clean energy industries, to crowd private investment...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero

writtenQuestions.heading: Coalfields Regeneration Trust: Finance
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Housing, Communities and Local Government, what progress she has made on considering the capital funding request from the Coalfields Regeneration Trust.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The fiscal position means that there have been tough choices to get us back on the path to recovery. It is in this context that the department is considering the request made for funding by the Coalfields Regeneration Trust. I was pleased to meet with And...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government

writtenQuestions.heading: Health Services: Finance
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Housing, Communities and Local Government, if she will provide a multi-year funding settlement for early intervention services in local government.
writtenQuestions.answerText: I refer the hon. Member to the answer given to Question UIN 40059 on 27 March 2025.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government

writtenQuestions.heading: Employment: Parents
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Work and Pensions, what recent assessment she has made of the adequacy of financial support provided to parents trying to enter back into the workforce that are currently in (a) education and (b) training.
writtenQuestions.answerText: An assessment has not been made on these parameters, however in DWP’s Get Britain Working White Paper, published November 2024, we set out our aspirations backed by a £55million investment for 2025/26, to transform Jobcentre Plus. This transformation will...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Work and Pensions
